svg.title: Armadillo Hotel Group Meraki topology
svg.desc: Two-property Meraki network — TX-ODE Odessa flagship hotel campus (247 devices across 5 buildings) and NM-CAR Carlsbad secondary property (14 devices). 261 devices total, dominated by 227 MR36 access points for guest-room coverage.
svg.text: Armadillo Hotel Group — Meraki multi-property · with physical device view
Cloud control plane · all device mgmt
5 buildings × 2 wings · ~230 guest-room APs · indoor + outdoor coverage
Service-wing layout · no Meraki wireless · 6 functional areas
• TX-ODE Odessa is the flagship — 247 of 261 devices (95%) live there, with proper core/access architecture (MS425 → MS125)
• 230 access points across 10 wings — averaging 23 MR36 per wing — suggests dense guest-room coverage
• Each building has 2× MS125-48FP (96 ports) feeding ~45 APs · oversubscription leaves room for IPTV, IoT, room control
• MG21 LTE failover at both sites — important for Carlsbad given remote location and limited terrestrial circuit options
• NM-CAR runs no Meraki wireless · either uses third-party APs or doesn't offer guest WiFi at this property
• MS120-8FP (FP = full PoE+) at Carlsbad enables PoE for IP phones and access control even on small access switches
